response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and extensive experience as a senior developer, they are fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is minimal (6 months at National Instruments, plus academic work) and absent from their current skills list, falling far short of the mandatory 5-10 years. They have zero domain expertise in fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking—the exact areas where the role demands demonstrated proficiency. Their career has consistently moved away from systems programming toward web, mobile, and infrastructure, making this a poor cultural fit and requiring extensive ramp-up in both C++ systems programming and financial services domain knowledge.
strengths: Proven track record of software craftsmanship, TDD practices, and building robust systems across multiple platforms
Strong communication and mentoring abilities, evidenced by workshops, teaching, and public speaking on engineering topics
Broad technical foundation and demonstrated ability to learn new technologies quickly across different domains
concerns: Severe deficit in C++ production experience (minimal exposure ~15 years ago); not even listed in current skills
Zero domain expertise in fixed income, capital markets, investment banking, or financial services—all areas central to the role
Career trajectory has consistently moved away from systems programming toward Node.js, web, and infrastructure work over the past 15 years

key_factors.factor: Absence of financial services domain knowledge
Strong engineering fundamentals and learning ability
